about.title: About
about.description: The Torah Rolling Assistant is designed for Gabbaim and Ba'alei Kriah to reduce Tircha d'Tzibbur. Given the current Torah position and the next reading, it calculates the exact rolling direction and number of columns required.
about.dataCreditsPrefix: The database for this project is based on open-source files from
about.dataCreditsMiddle: , which in turn utilize data from
about.dataCreditsThanks: Our thanks go to the tikkun.io project for sharing their work with the community.
about.issueInvite: If you have an improvement idea or wish to add a language, please open an issue on GitHub.
about.emailInvite: For direct contact, you are welcome to reach us by email.
about.dictaThanksSuffix: website for making their API available and for inspiring this project.

dicta.title: Dicta Illuminate
dicta.noResultSubtitle: Try again or search for a new source
dicta.idleSubtitle: Use the frame lines to align text before capture.
dicta.cameraUnavailable: Camera API is not available in this browser context.
dicta.cameraHttpsHint: Camera authorization is not available on localhost. Open the app over HTTPS instead of localhost.

instructions.1: Place the text so the line width stays inside the white frame
instructions.2: Use the yellow lines to align the image
instructions.3: Capture at least four lines of text

dicta.chooseSubtitle: Multiple matches were found. Select the correct page.

howTo.title: How To Use
howTo.subtitle: Follow these quick steps to get clear rolling instructions.

steps.step1: Identify the current location of the Torah scroll (the parasha it is currently open on) using the camera or manual input.
steps.step2: Select your target reading from the upcoming list or the full index.
steps.step3: The app calculates the rolling direction (Forward/Backward) and the number of columns to roll.

howTo.tip: Tip: update FROM first, then choose TO.

camera.title: Camera usage
camera.step1: Allow camera access when prompted by your browser.
camera.step2: Place the Torah text inside the frame in good, even lighting.
camera.step3: Hold steady for a moment so detection can lock correctly.
camera.step4: If detection fails, use manual input to set the current location.

tutorials.title: Guided tours on the live screen
tutorials.subtitle: These open on the Home page and point to the real parts of the app. Use the short tour for the main workflow, or the full tour to see every tool on the screen.

tutorialPrompt.title: New here?
tutorialPrompt.text: There are guided tours available from the How To page, and you can start the short one right now on the live screen.

step1.title: Open the language list
step1.description: Click or tap this language selector. The tour waits here so you can open the list yourself.

step2.title: Choose the language you want to use
step2.description: Pick a different language if you want. The tutorial text changes right away, or you can keep the current language and tap Next.

step3.title: Set where the Sefer Torah is open now
step3.description: This is the FROM side. Choose manually opens the full list of readings, the camera helps when you do not know where the Sefer is open, and Input is for when you already know the reference or tikkun page. The cards below show readings from the last month.

step4.title: Choose where you want to roll to
step4.description: This is the TO side. Most of the time you will choose one of the upcoming readings from the next month. The tour already picked an example so you can see the result.

step5.title: Read the result card before you roll
step5.description: This card shows the number of amudim to move, the direction to roll, and the FROM and TO pages used for the calculation.

step1.title: Open the language list
step1.description: Click or tap this language selector. The tour waits here so you can open the list yourself.

step2.title: Choose the language you want to use
step2.description: Pick a different language if you want. The tutorial text changes right away, or you can keep the current language and tap Next.

step3.title: This whole card is the FROM side
step3.description: Use this card to define where the Sefer Torah is open now. The three buttons at the top are the three ways to choose a reading, and the cards below show readings from the last month for quick selection.

step4.title: Choose manually opens the full reading list
step4.description: Click Choose manually when you want the full list of parashot, Yamim Tovim, Rosh Chodesh, fast days, and special readings.

step5.title: This overlay is the full reading index
step5.description: Search on top, then open the grouped sections below until you find the exact kriah you need.

step6.title: The camera button opens page recognition
step6.description: Click the camera when the Sefer Torah is already open and you want the site to identify the current place from the text on the klaf.

step7.title: This is the camera popup
step7.description: After the photo, the site tries to match the page and offer the most likely results for the current location.

step8.title: Input opens manual entry
step8.description: Click Input when you already know the reference or the tikkun page and want to enter it directly.

step9.title: This dialog lets you enter the exact place
step9.description: Set the sefer, perek, pasuk, or jump straight to a page number when you already know the exact location.

step10.title: The TO side is usually an upcoming reading
step10.description: This card is where you choose the reading you want to prepare next. Its calendar strip shows the upcoming month, and most of the time you will choose one of those next readings.

step11.title: Reference point lets you choose where TO begins
step11.description: Some readings can start from the beginning of the kriah, from a practical stopping point, or from the end of a partial section. These buttons choose which TO reference the roll should target.

step12.title: The result card is the main answer
step12.description: Once FROM and TO are set, this card gives the working answer: the number of amudim to roll, the direction, and the exact FROM and TO pages used for the calculation.

step13.title: This line appears when the roll crosses a book
step13.description: When you have to continue into a different chumash, this note tells you how many pages are still left after the end of the current book, for example after Exodus.

step14.title: Open the page preview from here
step14.description: Click the page number or the small book icon to open the preview for the selected page.

step15.title: The preview helps verify the page
step15.description: The preview shows the first line on the chosen page, lets you switch the nikud display, and gives a button to open the full page in a digital tikkun laid out like a regular Sefer Torah.

step16.title: The cog opens settings
step16.description: Click this cog to open the settings dialog.

step17.title: Settings control the community context
step17.description: Use settings to switch Israel or Gola mode, the nusach, and the scroll layout. These options affect which readings appear and how the roll is calculated.

step18.title: Use the navigation bar to reach the other pages
step18.description: On desktop the pages are in the top bar. On mobile, tap the menu button first to open the side navigation.

step19.title: About opens the contact page
step19.description: This is the About link. Finish the tour and the site will open the About page so you can see the contact details for the creator and developer, or report an issue.

help.gola: Enable this if you are outside Israel to synchronize the weekly Parasha readings and special Diaspora holiday readings with the Diaspora calendar.
help.nusach: Choose your reading tradition. Right now, only Sefaradic is available.
help.torahType: Most sifrei Torah use the רמ"ה layout (245 pages). Other layouts, like רמ"ח (248), can be added later.

result.title: Rolling Instructions
result.noData: Select both FROM and TO to compute roll

preview.lineUnavailable: First line is unavailable for this page.
preview.shiftTip: Tip: Hold Shift to toggle quickly

manual.title: Enter Location Manually
manual.page_not_found: Page not found for the selected reference.
